behavior_summary: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea (process) initializes the object's visual frame (frame 0) and starts a pulsing/zap routine by calling U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC when an actor enters the object's fast-area trigger.

U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC drives a timed sequence of frame changes and effects: it uses spawned timers (spawn -> FREE::func1C19 + suspend and urandom delays), updates Item frames, calls U8P_LITZAPNS::func01C3 to play a short SFX/frame pulse, and checks for overlapping items to apply randomized small damage to nearby NPCs via METHOD::receiveHitWrapper.

U8P_LITZAPNS::func01C3 is a short helper that plays a sound (playSFX) and advances the item's animation through a multi-frame sequence with short spawned waits; it implements the audible/visual pulse sub-sequence used by U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC.

U8P_LITZAPNS::leaveFastArea (process) clears/unregisters the object's fast-area reference by calling FREE::resetRef(0x06, this) when the actor leaves, effectively stopping or detaching the fast-area association.
notable_control_flow: Timing is implemented with spawn -> FREE::func1C19 and suspend; urandom is used to randomize some waits and damage values, producing non-deterministic timing/strength.

U8P_LITZAPNS::func01C3 contains a conditional sequence that steps frames from an initial index (inferred local1 = 0x03) while local1 <= 5, otherwise runs a fallback short sequence of frame changes — this shapes the visible pulse pattern.

Damage application in U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC iterates overlap results: it tests Item::isNpc and Npc::isDead before calling METHOD::receiveHitWrapper with a randomized damage amount and the target's direction (Npc::getDir).
open_questions: Exact numeric timing values and damage magnitudes applied by U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC (urandom ranges and spawn delay constants) are inferred but not precisely reconstructed.

Whether the pulse routine is a one-shot per U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea call or repeats periodically until some external condition is met (metadata suggests a single initiated routine that may loop internally).
Which actor type reliably triggers the fast-area (Avatar vs any actor) — the event is 'U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea' but the intended triggering actor is inferred from common use.
Whether any global flags or external state are toggled elsewhere to stop the pulse routine (no flags written in this script).

main_function.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: Start the object's pulse/zap visual state and begin the timed effect routine when an actor enters the object's fast-area trigger (set initial frame then invoke the pulse routine).
entry_conditions: Actor enters the object's fast-area trigger (engine invokes U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea event/process).

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area entry handler; initializes frame and launches the pulse routine

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC
function_map.role: primary pulse/zap utility: orchestrates timers, frame changes, calls SFX/frame subroutine, and applies hits to overlapping NPCs

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::func01C3
function_map.role: short SFX + multi-frame animation helper used during the pulse sequence

internal_calls.purpose: Kick off the pulse/zap routine after initializing the item's frame
Invoke the short audible/visual pulse sub-sequence (SFX + frame steps) as part of the larger timed effect

external_calls.purpose: Set the object's visual frame to an initial state (frame 0) when the fast-area is entered
Clear/unregister the object's stored reference for the fast-area (cleanup on exit)
Create short helper timer processes used in combination with suspend to implement delays
Generate randomized delays and randomized damage values to vary timing and hit strength
Update the object's frame during the pulse sequence to show different visual states
Iterate overlapping items/entities to find potential NPC targets in the pulse area
Verify an overlapped item is an NPC before attempting to damage it
Skip applying damage to NPCs that are already dead
Obtain the target NPC's facing/direction to supply orientation to the hit wrapper
Apply a small, randomized hit/damage to a valid, living overlapping NPC
Play the pulse sound effect that accompanies the visual frame changes
Spawn short timers between frame changes to pace the visual pulse
Pause execution until the spawned timer completes to time frame transitions

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea (process) initializes the object's visual frame (frame 0) and starts a pulsing/zap routine by calling U8P_LITZAPNS::func00BC when an actor enters the object's fast-area trigger.
